name: Londonderry Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Very sharp cheddar cheese, finely shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine flour, sugar, and grated lemon peel.
With a pastry blender or two knives used scissors fashion, cut in butter or margarine until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. With a fork, stir in 2 egg yolks and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla to form a very stiff dough. Chill for 30 minutes.
2. Preheat oven to 425°F.
Press one third of the chilled dough evenly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Bake for 8-10 minutes until golden brown. Cool.
3. Press the remaining dough around the sides of the pan, stopping 1 inch from the top. Do not bake.
4. In a large bowl, with the mixer on high speed, beat cream cheese until smooth.
Gradually add cheddar cheese, beating until well blended for about 5 minutes. Scrape the bowl occasionally with a rubber spatula.
5. At medium speed, gradually beat in 1/2 teaspoon grated lemon peel, 1/2 teaspoon vanilla, 1 3/4 cups sugar, 2 egg yolks, and remaining ingredients until blended.
Beat for 5 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
6. Pour the mixture into the crust and bake for 10 minutes. Turn the oven control to 300°F and bake an additional 1 hour and 15 minutes or until the top is firm when lightly pressed with a finger.
7. Cool in the pan on a wire rack. The cake will sink in the center, leaving cracks around the crust. With a spatula, loosen the pan sides and remove. Loosen the cake from the pan bottom; slide onto a plate; chill. Makes about 12 servings.
